mission.title: Install and Configure K3s (Lightweight Kubernetes)
mission.description: K3s is a lightweight, fully conformant Kubernetes distribution designed for resource-constrained environments. It is ideal for edge computing, IoT, and development scenarios where simplicity and low overhead are paramount. Key trade-offs include reduced complexity and resource usage at the potential cost of some advanced Kubernetes features.

steps.title: Install the K3s binary
steps.description:
This step installs the K3s binary on your machine, which is the core component of the K3s distribution.

```bash

curl -sfL https://get.k3s.io | sh -

```

> This command fetches and installs the latest K3s version.

steps.title: Verify K3s installation
steps.description:
Check that K3s is installed and running correctly by verifying the service status.

```bash

systemctl status k3s

```

> This command checks the status of the K3s service.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to allow kubectl to interact with your K3s cluster.

```bash

export KUBECONFIG=/etc/rancher/k3s/k3s.yaml

chmod 600 /etc/rancher/k3s/k3s.yaml

```

> This sets the KUBECONFIG environment variable to point to the K3s kubeconfig.
> This ensures that the kubeconfig file has the correct permissions.

steps.title: Verify nodes in the cluster
steps.description:
Ensure that the K3s node is registered and ready in the cluster.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> This command lists all nodes in the K3s cluster.

steps.title: Post-installation configuration
steps.description:
Set up additional features like metrics server and RBAC for enhanced functionality.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes-sigs/metrics-server/v0.5.0/deploy/1.16+/metrics-server-deployment.yaml

```

> This installs the metrics server for resource monitoring.

uninstall.title: Uninstall K3s
uninstall.description:
This step removes K3s and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all workloads and data stored in the cluster.

```bash

/usr/local/bin/k3s-uninstall.sh

```

> This script uninstalls K3s and cleans up the system.

upgrade.title: Upgrade K3s
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your current cluster state. This step performs the upgrade and verifies the installation.

```bash

kubectl get all --all-namespaces > k3s-backup-$(date +%F).yaml

curl -sfL https://get.k3s.io | sh -

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup all resources in the cluster.
> This command fetches and installs the latest version of K3s.
> Verify that all nodes are in a ready state after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'k3s is not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The K3s service may not have started correctly.

**Fix:**
Check the service logs with 'journalctl -u k3s' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig file may not be set up correctly or permissions may be incorrect.

**Fix:**
Ensure KUBECONFIG is set correctly and permissions are set with 'chmod 600 /etc/rancher/k3s/k3s.yaml'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no nodes available in cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The K3s node may not be ready or properly registered.

**Fix:**
Check node status with 'kubectl get nodes' and ensure the K3s service is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to start K3s'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient resources or conflicting services may prevent K3s from starting.

**Fix:**
Check system resources and logs for conflicts using 'journalctl -u k3s'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Kubernetes features and improved the metrics server integration.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecation of certain flags related to legacy storage drivers.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.30, ensure to review the new storage configurations.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get nodes' showing your node(s) in a 'Ready' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure your system meets the minimum requirements for running K3s.
